column58:
Cleaning the controller ventilation system. Confirm the controller ventilation system is not
dusty. If dust has accumulated, remove it.
column60:
Check for external damage or peeling paint. Check whether the robot has external damage or peeling paint due to the interference with the peripheral equipment. If an interference occurs, eliminate the cause. Also, if the external damage is serious, and causes a problem in which the robot will not operate, replace the damaged parts. (In case of M-10iD/12 dustproof wrist cover type)
â‡’â€œ7.2.5 Check of the wrist cover and the debris coverâ€
column62: Check for water. Check whether the robot is subjected to water or cutting oils. If water is found, remove the cause and wipe off the liquid.
column64: Check for damages to the teach pendant cable, the operation box connection cable or the robot connection cable. Check whether the cable connected to the teach pendant, operation box and robot are unevenly twisted or damaged. If damage is found, replace the damaged cables.
column66:
Check for damage to the mechanical unit cable (movable part) and welding cable. Observe the movable part of the mechanical unit cable and welding cable, and check for damage. Also, check whether the cables are excessively bent or unevenly twisted.
 â‡’â€œ7.2.3 Check the Mechanical Unit Cables and Connectorsâ€
column68: Check for damage to the end effector (hand) cable. Check whether the end effector cables are unevenly twisted or damaged. If damage is found, replace the damaged cables.
column70:
Check the connection of each axis motor and other exposed connectors. Check the connection of each axis motor and other exposed connectors.
â‡’â€œ7.2.3 Check the Mechanical Unit Cables and Connectorsâ€
column72:
Retightening the end effector mounting bolts. Retighten the end effector mounting bolts. Refer to the following section for tightening torque information:
â‡’â€œ4.1 END EFFECTOR INSTALLATION TO WRISTâ€
column74: Retightening the welding power cable mounting bolts. Retighten the welding power cable mounting bolts.
column76:
Retightening the external main bolts. Retighten the robot installation bolts, bolts to be removed for inspection, and bolts exposed to the outside. Refer to the recommended bolt tightening torque guidelines at the end of the manual. An adhesive to prevent bolts from loosening is applied to some bolts. If the bolts are tightened with greater than the recommended torque, the adhesive might be removed.
Therefore, follow the recommended bolt tightening torque guidelines when retightening the bolts.
column78:
Check the fixed mechanical stopper and the adjustable mechanical stopper. Check that there is no evidence of a collision on the fixed mechanical stopper, the adjustable mechanical stopper, and check that the stopper mounting bolts are not loose.
â‡’â€7.2.4 Check of Fixed Mechanical Stopper and Adjustable Mechanical Stopperâ€
column80:
Clean spatters, sawdust and dust. Check that spatters, sawdust, or dust does not exist
on the robot main body. If dust has accumulated, remove it. Especially, clean the robot movable parts well (each joint, J1 base inside and surroundings, around the welding torch, conduit part, wrist axis hollow part) Insulation failure might occur when the spatter has collected around the wrist flange or welding torch, and there is a possibility of damaging the robot mechanism by the welding current. (See Appendix C)

2/25/2026 - Arrived and proceeded to system 1 for inspection. Observed production run and witnessed fault generate of Product Pick Failure. Reset fault and watched production run until lunch break. Inspected system and found a loose fastener on the EOAT for one of the air cylinder controlling the tine clamps. Tightened all fasteners and paint marked them. Applied thin layer of lithium grease to tine contact points. Inspected robot pivot points. Robot is in overall very good health. Cleaned light curtains and inspected infeed conveyors. System is in good working order. 

Inspected System 1 during lunch break in production. Issue found with air fittings on pneumatic cylinder where 90 degree elbows are damaged, possibly from crash or from product rubbing. 
Inspected EOAT bolts and fasteners. All were tight. Light rubbing from Tines, but nothing extraordinary. Applied light layer of lithium grease to contact points for tines. Overall health is good!

column7:
Issue: Spray said a recipe was building incorrectly, but the recipe does not actually exist for the current product they are running.

Description: Spray came to us saying a pattern was not building right, upon further investigation the pattern for SKU 500628 was associated with Recipe 14, but this SKU did not match the case dimensions for that recipe despite it being included with that recipe in the project summary.

Resolution: I am making a new Recipe (Recipe 22) which will have the same pattern as recipe 14, but will accommodate the taller case dimension.

Update 11/6/25: Recipe has been made and I added it to the folder for the system, but have not heard from the customer to schedule testing.

column7:
2/18 - Arrived on site and met with Tom the maintenance manager. He accompanied me to the systems and explained a few issues they have been experiencing. One complaint was the pick quality on their smallest box (3oz 7pack). I inspected the system and found a small leak on J6. Will monitor for future possible replacement. I replaced the foam pads on the EOAT with customer stock pads. Cleaned debris off conveyor and centering device and inspected paint markings on fasteners for any movement. All checked good. Removed tape from infeed conveyor rollers. Observed system stack 3oz 12pack boxes, only encountering issues when there was a problem upstream with taping. 

Inspected system 1 at wyandot snacks. Checked dress pack and watched production. Found dress pack was again disconnected at the last bracket. I secured the dress pack and found tension when the robot was placing boxes in the right station, stack nearest the HMI (left corner near operator). I moved the dress pack down 1 inch using slack from J3 and the issue resolved. Watched multiple pallets on both sides stack without issue. Removed tape from foam pad on EOAT.

2/18/2026 - Performed inspection on System 2. Customer had issues with conveyor stopping and starting after robot picked a box from the conveyor. I observed the system and noticed the conveyor control module was faulting after the centering device retracted. I inspected the control module and found the comms link cable to be extremely loose. The vibration from the centering device was enough for comms to drop momentarily. I tightened the connection and system ran without issue. I replaced the worn pads on the EOAT. Customer also stated there was an issue with the dress pack coming loose, breaking the plastic base that holds the mastering check prongs on the EOAT. They had secured the broken base but do need a replacement sent.

2/18/2026 - Customer had described issues with boxes being kicked sideways and even 180 degrees coming off of the divert for both SYS1 and SYS2. I made minor adjustments to the divert banks, lowering speeds from 1.7 m/s eventually down to 1.3 m/s to more closely match the parent conveyor speeds. Boxes no longer kick sideways. Clayton at Wyandot is familiar with adjusting the conveyor and he will monitor for any further necessary adjustments. Inspected Conveyor, cleaned photo eyes, removed tape from rollers, blew off dust accumulating on the divert banks. Conveyor is running smoothly. Customer would like a solution for removing buildup from the divert rollers.

Issue: High Performance Power Supply, faulting out curve conveyor

Description: Conveyor not running due to over current fault on Power Supply

Resolution:  AC side: measured 269 leg 1, leg 2 measured 269 and leg 3 measured 267, and measured 469 between line 1 and line 3,  AC side good.
DC side: Block 1 measured 2.1vdc, Block 2 measured 2.2vdc, I think we have a bad High Performance Power Supply, I notice switch was faulty also, waiting for new unit should before 9:00am according to tracking. Swapped out old HP Power Supply with new, add jumper in the 25amp slot

column3: SMS vacuum is not picking tilesProduction Support WO
column7:
Issue: The robot was not picking the tiles.

Findings:
I checked the logic and confirmed that all I/O, AR, and GI/GO signals were running properly. The issue was traced to the Coval vacuum modules.

Resolution:
I adjusted the blow regulator to ensure the Coval module generated the proper grip signal.
I also installed indicators to prevent overfilling the magazine with product.

Monitoring:
I monitored the system for 2 hours and everything ran correctly.

Issue: 
Juan Gomez called it and said the robot collided when placing a box and once he reset the robot collided with the frame. Robot is not able to be reset or sent home. Juan is sending some pictures now.
Description:

Resolution: SL updated the software to resolve pallet removal issues; SL advised deleting two background programs that were messing with how the robot performed moves.

SL contacted Yaskawa several times until they identified the root cause of the S1C1G0335 parameter changing.

I supported the troubleshooting efforts onsite.

I deleted the MotoROS.out file from the robot controller.

I updated the robot’s time to match Chicago’s local time.

We attempted to recreate the fault with several power cycles, but the issue did not reoccur.

We tested dry cycles and ran product through the cell, and the system is currently running well.

column3: <Corrective Remote Love Beets Robot CPM (Virtual Support)>
column7:
Issue: Robot not keeping up with the infeed

Description:

I am seeing a good amount of operator intervention due to misplaced boxes leading to the infeed getting backed up. Going to work on adjusting a few things to get cleaner box placement

Adjusted the height from 150mm to 154mm in effort to avoid the robot from knocking boxes off the pallet on the right side. This caused the left side to drop boxes on the pallet rather then place them.
Spoke with Trevor who said he remembers a floor gradient between the left and the right side.
Reverted the box height change and instead adjusted the overall speed and time to place.
Trevor is digging into implementing the scaling offset for placement height which would account for the sloped floor.
Nevertheless, Please see images for left and right side rates. I also timed it using playback and got a rate of about 9 CPM.

Resolution:
Robot speed and robot time to place a box has been increased. Robot time to place a box will hopefully result in less knocked over boxes / operator intervention. Adjustments have been communicated with Steve Martino. The importance of the operator placing the slipsheet or correcting a knocked over box and resetting safety efficiently has also been communicated with Steve.
case rate this morning:
Left side: 9.6 cpm
right side: 7.46 cpm

Message sent to Steve Martino:
Hey Steve. This is Brad from Formic. I have not been able to get ahold of mark who inquired about speeding up the robot to keep up with the infeed. Feel free to share his email and I would be happy to copy this over. Our adjustments today were in efforts to increase the case rate that the robot would palletize. We did cause more down time than expected but hopefully for a better long term result. 

A few things I noticed when watching production before adjustment:
-production is delayed by slip sheet placement 
-production is delayed by operator correction, meaning when the robot drops a box the operator goes in and removes the box. 

Adjustments we have made:
-we have increased the movement speed of the robot
-we have increased the time that the robot has to place a box to avoid knocked/drop over boxes in the future 

Other than that,
It will help the robot keep up with the infeed if the operators try to place the slip sheet or correct a robot error efficiently and resume production.

column3: SMS robot colliding tiles in press - Corrective
column7:
Issue:
The customer reported that the robot was colliding with tiles inside the press.

Description:
Tiles were bouncing and entering the press inconsistently, causing the robot to smash the tiles during placement.

Resolution:
I increased the Z offset on the first bend, Linear Position 22, from 20 to 28. After this adjustment, the robot stopped colliding with the tiles in the press.
Due to press-related issues, extended testing was limited; however, the system has been running for over 4 hours without issues. I am continuing to monitor, and the system appears to be operating properly.

Next Actions / Follow-Up:
A new work order is required to address an air leak caused by a PLC signal that enables the vacuum cups on the right side of the elevator. These vacuum cups are not used in the current process and are unnecessarily being energized.
I will submit a request with CNC Solutions to begin working on eliminating this air leak.

column3: Pharmaceutical Specialties - BeRobox Support - Corrective Remote WO - FS - 472
column7:
Issue: Robot is stuck halfway between last pick and home position.

Description: The robot placed a box on the pallet and then stayed there sitting above the box. With some intervention by the operator, the arm attempted to return back to home, but got stuck again halfway there (photos of current position attached). There was no collision with a foreign object or obstacle. All sensor lenses are clean and have a green "go" light illuminated. We also checked the control panel and there are no RED LED's or loose connections. 

Now, it is still stuck in that position, and we are unable to turn the servo motors on or jog the arm. I have attached pictures of the errors/alarms as well as the serial number of the robot. 

Email sent to BeRobox for support. 

Resolution: BeRobox Support responded back and suggested J6 was either jogged or guided outside of it's joint limits. They recommend we follow the recovery steps on page 14 of the manual to guide that joint back into tolerance range. 

Upon calling the customer back they informed us they are not planning to use the robot for the rest of the day today and are unable to assist with recovery at the moment. Customer would like a call back on Monday morning to handle this.

12/22 Resolution: Walked the customer through "recovery mode", allowing them to turn the servos on and jog J6 back into tolerance. J6 had gone to -366 degrees and was rotated back to the range within -360 to 360 degrees. After that the robot was homed and they resumed picking.

column3: Trident - Support Call - Corrective Remote WO
column7:
Issue: After a product changeover the customer stated the robot was stacking the boxes incorrectly (starting in the middle of the pallet). Customer also stated the tines were retracting too fast, allowing the bottom of the un-taped box to fall down a little bit before being placed.

Description: Call taken at 4:30AM Saturday 12/20. Went over a few parameters with the customer confirming the correct pallet was chosen for the product and that the robot was performing a pallet scan. Also went through expected product dimensions and build pattern.

Resolution: Determined the customer had selected the wrong pattern for the product they were picking. They had chosen 143808, when they should have selected 143811. This change corrected the pattern issue. To correct the placing issue, I had the customer increase place time by increments of 0.01 to allow the gripper to stay engaged until the box was fully placed. Customer ended up increasing place time from 0.09 to 0.11.

Issue:
	•	AMS auger was not running
	•	XY table went into fault
	•	PRIO230 and PRIO231 Ethernet alarms

Description:
	•	The hopper contained highly compacted material
	•	The XY table went out of sequence
	•	The Ethernet board and some controller connectors were not functioning properly

Resolution:
	•	Rootbeer and I performed troubleshooting on the system
	•	We emptied the hopper to prevent the auger from getting stuck. The full sensor in the hopper needs to be replaced.(I will order it)
	•	Rootbeer assisted with resetting the XY table
	•	After extensive troubleshooting, we identified a faulty connector inside the controller. I cleaned it and checked all connections. After this, the system was restored and is running properly.

Recipe Addition Required
Same as recipe 543A except height is 155.5mm
8 layers.

column3: PMA XY table down- Description - Corrective (On site)
column7:
Issue:
XY table positions were incorrect.

Description:
The belts were heavily contaminated with dust, and material buildup was found in the belt teeth, affecting positioning accuracy.

Resolution:
I disassembled the XY table to thoroughly clean the belts and pulleys. With support from Rootbeer, we re-taught the Home position in the system and troubleshot an encoder issue caused by a loose table.

Issue: costumer reported the robot was missing picks because the robot was not going all the way down.

Description: Robot has the alarm “part not picked”

Resolution:

Recipie Adjustment Required
I worked with Todd today while they were running 5 acres 32 oz. I made a slight adjustment to help placement because it was lowering the box too much (increased product height by 6mm). This is an important note because it could have an impact on box picks but it has been good so far.

The robot stayed on the left side because after a box was not sealed on the bottom, product dropped out of a box. One operator went in, picked up the dropped product and then started taking boxes from the conveyor to finish the stack on the pallet. Another operator started the machine but the robot was not aware of the boxes that the first operator put on there causing it to collide with the top of the stack
I communicated the sequence of events to Todd from sunrise

Issue: Customer is experiencing a vacuum verification failure.

Description: They swapped out the vacuum relay and it worked for 1 hour before failing again. Additional attempts to resolve include rebooting the system, resetting the vacuum generator, and cleaning the filters. Vacuum pressure reads ~ -68 on both gauges when picking successfully. When it fails, only one gauge registers ~ -68 (LED turns red) while the other stays at ~0 (LED stays green). This may be an indication that the suction cups on that air path aren't fully seating on the box and not generating vacuum.
Air lines are all secure, suction cups are free of any debris and are clean.

Resolution: Issue was ONLY present for a recipe they created for double picking. Singles and authorized recipes are fine.

column3: Roller Drive 4 bad at Wyandot Sys2
column7:
Roller Drive 4 on the infeed to System 2 at is bad. Root cause of the problem was a missing nut on the threaded portion of the conveyor roller drive. The drive spun itself and the cable wrapped itself tight enough to damage the wires inside the pigtail. I was able to source a nut from the old conveyor drive roller interior and use it to fasten the new roller drive to the conveyor frame. Watched multiple cycles successfully pass through. 

-Other issues on site include formic FPI screen errors. Forwarded to Jared Wagler. 
-Customer still has issues with the divert on the main conveyor.

column7:
Issue:
Customer is noting there is occasionally "too much pressure" when the robot attempts to pick up a box from the conveyor. Appears to be related to physical pressure (not air pressure), as the robot is treating it like a collision. Issue started earlier today and is occurring more and more as the day goes on.

When the issue happens, the customer presses "home" and robot moves back to home position and is then able to resume picking.

Description:
The EAOT sensor signals were experiencing a delay signals coming from the Baluff module.

Resolution:
With support from Tyler, we performed troubleshooting and identified an Ethernet cable that was not properly connected. After securing the connection, the fault was cleared and the system returned to normal operation.

column3: Pridgeon and clay - servos on, program not running - Corrective (On site)
column7:
Issue: sys1 has error “Servos on, program not running” and will not run the mastering or brake test. Faults also included Master and brake check needed. Had the customer jog the robot back into the light curtains and attempted to run the program. Fault of Servos on, Program not running persisted. Worked with Israel Perez via remote support to select the program while in External mode. Faults persisted. It was determined the best course of action would be to travel to P&C the morning of 1/10. I arrived at P&C and the building was closed with no cars in the parking lot. I went to the other location and spoke with the maintenance team. They could not spare someone to go and stay with me while I troubleshot the systems. Traveled back home and informed Tyler (the original contact for the trouble call) of the situation and that we will resume remote troubleshooting on Monday morning when the plant resumes production. 

Description:

Resolution: Compressor for system air was not on. Grippers could not cycle to initiate the program. Eric Voss was able to resolve the issue and the systems are up and running production.

Issue:
Kyle Conrad reported that the AMS system on their robotic cell lost connection and is unresponsive to the pendant, requiring assistance to reset and reconnect it to the PLC.Kyle also mentioned that the augur Insider AMS is due for replacement and that there is no spare available in their cabinet, referencing a previous discussion about this issue three months ago.

Description:
The AMS XY table was down because the Y servo drive was in a Major Fault. The root cause was mechanical binding: the pulleys had solidified product packed into the teeth, causing the axis to physically get stuck. During inspection, I found that the bearings and rails were damaged and will need to be replaced.

Resolution:
Fully disassembled the XY table to perform a complete cleaning. Reassembled the system, re-taught home, reset faults, and restarted the system.
Additionally, the auger was replaced.

column3: Georgia Nut - Area Scanner Er 52 - Corrective (On site)
column7:
Issue: Juan called in that the SYS2 area scanner had fault “er 52” that locked the system in slow mode.

Description: Area scanner power connection was loose

Resolution: Tighten connection.

Issue: Grippers opening too far when placing product on the pallet. 

Description: The recipe in reference is Product 15 on the TP. Original place time was 0.11 seconds. I worked with customer and observed on camera while adjusting. Adjusted place time down to .08, .06 and finally to .04 before product stopped being shoved. Customer has concerns about gaps still, but I explained this is inherent in the style of gripper along with product dimensions compared to pallet size. Will continue to monitor and adjust where feasible.

Resolution: Adjusted the place time, incrementally, down to 0.04 from 0.11 on recipe 16oz Dogfood P10348 (Product 15).

Issue: Servo power on, program not running

Description: customer switched recipes and received an error of servos on, program not running. Investigated floor pallet lasers and EOAT laser. Secure. Spoke with Brian at FOCUS. 

Resolution:
Message from FOCUS "What has happened is a bug if they change product before letting it scan a new pattern, while changing to a pattern with less picks."

Resolution: "We can set fosindex[1].pick, fosindex[1].layer, fosindex[2].pick, fosindex[2].layer to 1, and .complete on both to true to force them to scan again. This will fix the issue we have right in front of us, we can discuss changes to keep it from happening again."

Issue: 
Customer was not able to realign the light curtains for the robot area.

Description:

1. Light curtains were very dirty

2. Left bay light curtain dirty and misaligned

3. Right bay light curtain was cleaned and realigned but when I shook the fence, it would come out of realignment since the right bay fence was secured to the slip sheet fence with brackets

Resolution:

1. Cleaned the light curtains. Left a few microfiber rags behind for the customer.

2. Cleaned and realigned

3. Removed bracket connecting the right bay fence and the slip sheet fence. Lowered the top bracket for the LC closest to the slip sheet area so that it could have a better alignment. Added double sided sticky tape for extra security. Durability will be monitored.

Communicating with the maintenance staff to clean the lens of the LCs with the microfiber rags left behind and to remind the operators to do their best to not hit the light curtains.

Issue: Appears they have made an improvement to their mold...... this changed the behavior of the pick in the IMM.  

Description: This fix is not something one should do remotely. Likely fix will need eyes on the ground with support ready in background. Need to possibly make an intermediate move or YPR adjustment to the current move. Part used to curl waiting to be picked. Now comes out straight and our move curls it down. 

The robot is dropping the sprew at an incorrect location. Possible robot point adjustment needed.

Resolution: Sprews are now pointing straight out at a 90 degree angle and no longer have a curve (which the original mapping accounted for). A new pickapproachsprew position was added to the logic which accounts for the straight on approach.

column3: Love Beets - Crowder Extensions Install - Corrective (On site)
column7:
System 3 Corrective Action – Box Aligner & Stacking Quality

There was an issue with the box aligner not fully closing on boxes, resulting in poor stacking quality.

Corrective Action Taken:

Installed crowder extension plates.

Result: The crowder now fully closes on the boxes and properly aligns them for pick.

This led to significant improvement in stacking; however, we still observed occasional boxes falling off the stack.

Root Cause Identified:

The robot was positioned too high above the previous layer before release.

Resolution:

Adjusted the Z-offset for both the left and right sides.

This required careful tuning due to uneven floor conditions (high and low spots).

I optimized the offset to prevent collisions on high spots while also preventing excessive drop height on low spots.

After completing adjustments, I monitored pallet stacking for two pallets per side. No boxes fell during monitoring. Stacking performance was stable and significantly improved.

Original Z offset
Left side:
-738
Right side:
-730

Modified Z offset
Left side:
-762
Right side:
-736

Additional Recommendation

Stacking quality could improve further if the boxes were taped tighter to ensure they remain more square. Boxes that are slightly bowed or not fully squared can affect alignment consistency and overall pallet stability. Improving box squareness would help maximize the effectiveness of the recent adjustments.

Confirmed air being supplied at the inlet

Made sure the soft start knob was not turned all the way to the "slow" direction. While verifying the customer turned it a very small amount towards the slow direction and then the system started getting air.

column7:
Issue:
Unsuccessful pick when running the green cases
Clamp with bottom support fingers not fully actuating open

Root cause:
1. Disconnected Airline (Immediate Failure)
-Caused clamp side plate not to actuate fully.
-Resulted in incomplete opening/closing
-Resolved by reconnecting airline.

2. Incorrect Case Size & Recipe (Primary Ongoing Issue)
-Customer was running a new green case not included in original project scope.
-Minimum EOAT clamp width: 191.5–192.51 mm
-Green case width (TRF measurement): 185 mm
-Customer measurement: 190.5 mm
-Selected recipe width setpoint: 199 mm

The green case width is below the EOAT’s minimum closing stroke. Therefore:
-Clamp cannot fully close on box.
-Missed picks occur due to poor clamping.
-Incorrect recipe caused improper box placement.
-Continued operation would result in repeat pick failures.

TRF confirmed that this case was previously identified as incompatible with the current EOAT design. A new EOAT is already in development to support this case size.

Resolution:
-Reconnected airline to restore clamp actuation.
-Advised customer not to run the green case configuration with current EOAT.
-Communicated that no validated recipe exists for this box due to mechanical limitations.
-Reinforced requirement to revert any mechanical modifications to EOAT.
-EOAT upgrade scheduled for first week of March to support new case format.

column3: Pioneer travel and On site support

column7:
Customer reports the chuck on the lathe is not opening when the robot is picking from the lathe. Open signal is sent multiple times before it opens.

Arrived on site and was able to duplicate the issue. Robot would be stuck waiting for the lathe chuck to release so it could remove the part. I observed the robot run and, contrary to previous information, the robot was sending the foot pedal output to toggle the chuck on every cycle. 

I tested the unit by putting it in teach and rapidly toggling the DO that controls the foot pedal. It would always toggle on and off but performance of the chuck was spotty. I investigated the wiring inside the lathe and located an ice cube relay that tied to the I/O card on the door. The I/O card would toggle just fine, but the relay would sometimes fail to switch fully. 

I trained the customer on replacing the relay and they said they were happy to replace the relay when it arrives. Ordered relay through spare parts and verified it shipped. Will follow up with Tommy to see how the install went.

column7:
Issue: The “gripper failed reset test”  alarm is active. There is nothing causing the gripper to get stuck physically.

Description: Coment from Brian: “The issue here I think is that it failed the reset test for some reason after a power cycle. they let it sit for more than 2 hours though, which is longer than kuka allows from power up to mastering test, so it failed that test. If it attempted the test though, it passed the gripper test, so that issue should be fine”

Resolution: Spoke to Juan and got some more context.

They have been dealing with this issue for going on two weeks now. The robot will pick ~3 cases and then get the error case was not picked correctly . When they reset it from this error the EoAT collides with the conveyor. They have confirmed the tine sensors are positioned correctly and illuminate red corresponding with open and closed positions.

He is also concerned that in the closed position the tines do not adequately reach the underside of the box. He confirmed the tines are not physically obstructed by anything.

Due to extensive troubleshooting on site from their end they are requesting a site visit.

I'm booking travel now.

column3: ISR-1293: Venus Labs - Gripper Failed Errors - On Site
column7:
Issue: They have been dealing with this issue for going on two weeks now. The robot will pick ~3 cases and then get the error "case was not picked correctly".

Description: They have been dealing with this issue for going on two weeks now. The robot will pick ~3 cases and then get the error case was not picked correctly . When they reset it from this error the EoAT collides with the conveyor. They have confirmed the tine sensors are positioned correctly and illuminate red corresponding with open and closed positions.

He is also concerned that in the closed position the tines do not adequately reach the underside of the box. He confirmed the tines are not physically obstructed by anything.

Due to extensive troubleshooting on site from their end they are requesting a site visit.

Resolution: 

Adjustments made to:

-Pneumatic actuators to allow tines to fully close
-Tine sensors
-Left-right position of tines to avoid conveyor rollers
-Conveyor guide rails
-Conveyor sensors
-Adjusted slipsheet pick speed PTP to avoid double picking

Fix conveyor roller that fell out of position and realign guide rails and sensors due to them being loose. 
Monitored multiple pallets to validate changes.

column3: Harvest - Case Packer Cylinder Jam and System not Resetting - Corrective (On site)
column7:
Issue: Harvest escalated two different issues- cylinder R1PX-1 jam and, later, case Packer not resetting.

Description: Cylinder jam was caused by cylinders not connected to the correct solenoids. Failing to reset was due to first 4 sensors coming out of the kitchen having all their values set to -99.

Resolution: Correctly connect cylinders to appropriate solenoids, label all critical connections and set the sensors to appropriate range between 30-60

column3: Coastal Wipers - Guide Rail Install / Reteach Pick Position - Corrective (On site)
column7:
Description:

Boxes are catching on the conveyor side rails causing them to rotate

Plan of Action:

-install a guide rail on the side that the boxes are locating to for the pick position.
-Guiderails will only protrude from the conveyor rail by the width of the guiderail (0.71") this is make it so no adjustment is needed from recipe to recipe
-Flare the upstream end of the guide rail so there is a smooth transition between the existing and new guiderail sections
-Reprogram the pick positions (offset to the width of the new guiderail)
See attached drawing for reference. Blue line is the existing guide rail and the yellow line would be the new guide rail

***Also install SD cards and inspect airline replacement

Resolution:

-installed SD cards
-inspected airline replacement
-installed conveyor guide rails 
-adjust Y pick offset to account for conveyor guide rails

Original Y pick offsets:
Right build (narrow side leading):
-76mm
Left build (narrow side leading):
85mm

New Y pick offsets:
Right build (narrow side leading):
-90mm
Left build (narrow side leading):
108mm

column3: CAAP - Adjust Home Positions / Adjust Pallet A / Pallet B User Coordinates - Corrective (On site)
column7:
Issue: 

1. The home position was reset to 0 for all joints (arm straight up when manually jogged to home). Customer would like a way to manually jog the robot home. For now, if the customer needs to recover the arm they jog the robot near the home position and start the program. I spoke to Berobox about this issue. They communicated that this is a known bug with Doosan robots. They let me know how to correct the home position.

2. EOAT is not square to the conveyor / box / pallet. Stack quality could be improved by adjusting this. Spoke to Berobox about this one as well. They recommended that I adjust the frame of Conveyor A, in particular the C angle. Increments of +0.050 should have an effect.

Resolution:

1. New home position has been set and tested

2. Here are the images of the first layer centered on pallet B (right side). Unfortunately I did not take pictures of pallet A (left side). Placement is the same as pallet B and customer was happy with the changes. Pallet A / Pallet B coordinates were adjusted, not the conveyor frame.

column3: Onsite corrective + Replacement Cable Order for Boxed Water
column7:
Cable on dress pack is worn through. Needs Replacement sent ASAP.

-3/5/2026 - traveled to boxed water stopping to get a cable for end of arm tooling for emergency backup. Proceeded to Customer site and met with Nick. He showed me to the system and we evaluated the damage on the cables. TRF arrived shortly after and they brought replacement cables and airlines. They believe the issue stems from picking a slip sheet, causing excess rotation on joint six. TRF removed the end of arm tooling and replaced all cables and airlines. TRF finished their work on Friday. Customer is back in production. TRF also installed protection to help guard cables and airlines against rubbing in the future. They will add this to all future dress pack upgrades. Slip sheet pick was updated to reduce range of motion on J6.
